docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Shared publishing space

Liminal Signal

Short essays, reflections, and field notes from across the org.

Posts publish when they are marked ready, keeping the feed calm and deliberate.

View posts Start here Contributor owned Editor reviewed Markdown first Signal board What is moving Drafts Field notes in progress Working sessions and rough sketches Review Peer edits and revisions Feedback cycles before release Ready Published dispatches Short essays and experiment recaps All posts

docs.title: Essentials
docs.text: Intro and setup Get your environment ready and run the first build. Weekly labs Step-by-step exercises with checkpoints and rubrics. Course guide Policies, grading, and how submissions work. Team Contributors, editors, and contact details. Mixed media Photo dumps, sketches, and fragments.

docs.title: How we publish
docs.text: Prepare Short reading or video to frame the lab. Build Hands-on work in small teams or pairs. Publish Push work through PRs for review and feedback. Reflect Write a short note on what broke and what stuck.

docs.title: Contribute
docs.text: Open a pull request or leave a note in issues. View posts

docs.title: About the project
docs.text: Liminal Signal is a shared publication space for the org. We publish short essays, reflections, and field notes from across projects.

docs.title: What lives here
docs.text:
• Contributor writing and reflections
  • Project notes and progress updates
  • Announcements and resources
  • Media experiments and prototypes

docs.title: How we work
docs.text:
• Each contributor owns a post and updates it as needed.
  • Editors review changes through pull requests.
  • Questions, feedback, and requests happen in GitHub issues.

docs.title: Feature Examples
docs.text:
Explore some of the cool features supported by this site:

  • Admonitions
  • Code Highlighting
  • Math Rendering
  • Interactive Elements

docs.title: Admonitions
docs.text:
Note

This is a simple note.

Warning

Be careful with warnings!

Expandable

Details can be hidden inside collapsible tips.

docs.title: Code Highlighting
docs.text:
def greet(name):
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

echo "Hello from the shell"

Inline code like print("hi") is also supported.

docs.title: Task List
docs.text:
• Write docs
  • Deploy site

docs.title: Tabs
Details
docs.text:
Click to expand

Hidden content inside a collapsible block.

docs.title: Emoji and Keys
docs.text: Press ++Ctrl+Shift+P++ to open the command palette.

docs.title: Math Rendering
docs.text:
Inline math: \(E=mc^2\)

Block math:

\[ \int_0^1 x^2 dx = \frac{1}{3} \]

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This section documents the API endpoints available in the project.

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text:
• GET /items: List all items
  • POST /items: Create a new item
  • DELETE /items/{id}: Delete an item

docs.title: Policies
docs.text:
• Attend labs prepared and on time.
  • Late work is accepted up to 48 hours with a 10 percent deduction.
  • Collaboration is encouraged, but every write-up and Op-Ed is written individually.

docs.title: Grading
docs.text:
• Labs and checkpoints: 40 percent
  • Writing and Op-Eds: 30 percent
  • Final project and presentation: 30 percent

docs.title: Submissions
docs.text:
• All submissions happen through pull requests.
  • Use one PR per assignment and include the assignment name in the title.
  • Required reviews come from code owners or the editor team.

docs.title: Weekly Labs
docs.text: Labs focus on building, documenting, and reviewing work as a team.

docs.title: Lab flow
docs.text:
• Read the prompt and identify the deliverables.
  • Build a small milestone before expanding.
  • Document decisions and tradeoffs in your post.
  • Submit through a PR and respond to feedback.

docs.title: Extras
docs.text: Here you'll find additional tips, tricks, and optional tools to enhance your workflow.

docs.title: Includes
docs.text:
• Recommended extensions
  • Debugging tips
  • Community links

docs.title: Intro and Setup
docs.text: This guide helps you get the repository running and make your first post.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• Clone the repo and install dependencies
  • Run mkdocs serve to preview the site
  • Create a branch and edit your post file
  • Open a pull request for review

docs.title: First post workflow
docs.text:
1. Create a branch named week-01/your-name.
  2. Edit your file in docs/blog/posts/.
  3. Commit and push your changes.
  4. Open a PR and request review.
